reason: Appropriations bills are high-signal Prowler targets: they are primary vectors for fiscal bleed, rider insertion, and agency-level regulatory capture. Without summary details, the absence of transparency itself warrants investigation—appropriations acts routinely embed carve-outs and exemptions that obscure their commercial payload.
vectors: Fiscal bleed: appropriations mechanics enable debt-limit gaming and TGA flow redirection
Regulatory capture: financial services agencies (OCC, SEC, CFPB, FRB) are common targets for safe-harbor riders and exemption language
Hidden riders: general government appropriations frequently contain non-germane legislative payloads
Agency budget hostage-taking: appropriations conditionality used to defund enforcement actions
title: Financial Services and General Government Appropriations Act, 2027

reason: RIC income classification hack targeting precious metals funds. Classic regulatory capture vector: redefines 'qualifying income' to protect specific financial product structures from excise taxation, artificially preserves tax-advantaged status for positioned fund managers.
title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to provide that income received by a regulated investment company from precious metals shall be treated as qualifying income.
